evals.prompt: Set up a dashboard for my budget worker. It has logs/out.log, memory/expenses.json (array of {date, amount, category}), memory/stats.json ({total_month: 312.50, budget: 500}), and memory/summary.md.
evals.expected_output: hive.toml [comb] section with: log cell for logs/out.log, markdown cell for memory/summary.md, metric cells for total_month and budget from stats.json, table cell for expenses.json. Sensible titles. hive comb restart reminder.

evals.prompt: I want a dashboard for my habit tracker. memory/health.json has {status: 'ok'}, memory/streaks.json has {meditation: 14, exercise: 3}, and I want to be able to browse and filter my habit log interactively.
evals.expected_output: hive.toml [comb] section with: status cell for health.json, metric cells for each streak key, an app cell for the interactive log browser. Scaffolds dashboard/habit_browser.py with make_router() that reads memory/, renders an HTML page with the habit data. Notes that only Hive env packages are available.

evals.prompt: Just add a basic dashboard for my worker — it only has a log file and a summary.md in memory.
evals.expected_output: Minimal [comb] section with just two cells: log cell for logs/out.log and markdown cell for memory/summary.md. No over-engineering.
